function.name: mcp_agentcall_configure_inbound_ai
function.description:
Set up an AI voice agent to autonomously answer incoming calls to a phone number. This is the AgentCall equivalent of Twilio's inbound voice webhook URL: instead of pointing the number at a webhook you host, you give AgentCall a system prompt and AgentCall handles the call with a managed AI voice agent. Use this (NOT 'create_webhook') when you want a phone number to answer calls. Set the system prompt, voice, and optional first message; when someone calls the number, the AI picks up and follows the prompt. Pass `notify.emailTo` to also receive a plain-English summary of every call (caller, intent, urgency, callback time) so small-business owners do not need to read transcripts. Free tier: 5 minutes/month free trial, no card required. Once the trial is exhausted, inbound AI calls hang up until the 1st of next month (UTC) or until the user upgrades to Pro for unlimited inbound AI at $0.40/min. Available on US and Canada numbers. Before writing systemPrompt from scratch, fetch GET https://api.agentcall.co/v1/calls/prompt-templates (no auth) for pre-built templates: receptionist, lead qualifier, appointment booker, customer support, call screener. Preview voices via GET https://api.agentcall.co/v1/calls/voices.

IMPORTANT (persistent config: confirm before enabling, monitor after): this is NOT a one-shot tool call. Once configured, the number autonomously answers every incoming call from then on, accumulates per-minute charges (at $0.40/min on Pro past the Free 5-minute monthly trial), and keeps doing so until disable_inbound_ai is called. Before invoking, get the user's explicit confirmation on: (1) exact E.164 number to configure (read it back), (2) systemPrompt content (read back at least the WHAT WE DO and PRICING sections so the user can correct invented facts before any caller hears them), (3) record setting (default false; recording adds $0.01/min and 1-year retention), (4) notify.emailTo (confirm the address is theirs and that they want every call summarized), (5) maxDurationSecs (use the smallest value that fits the use case so a stuck call cannot run for an hour), (6) budget expectations (Free 5 min/month then hang-up, or Pro $0.40/min with no monthly cap). After enabling, periodically check get_usage so the user sees consumption, and proactively offer to call disable_inbound_ai when their stated goal is met (testing finished, campaign over, business closed for the season). On reports of unexpected charges or unexpected calls, disable_inbound_ai first then debug.

systemPrompt.description: Instructions for the AI when it answers (e.g. 'You are the front desk for Acme Plumbing. Greet callers, take their name, phone, and a brief description of the issue, then say someone will call back within 2 hours.')

voice.description: Voice: 10 options. marin (soft, natural) and cedar (warm, grounded) are the newest natural-sounding picks. shimmer (default), sage, ash, ballad, coral, echo, verse, alloy. Preview at GET /v1/calls/voices.

Tell the user to make sure their first message includes a TCPA disclosure ('this call may be recorded for quality') — required by two-party-consent states. Default false.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

REPLACE MODE (mode='replace', default): find a unique string and replace it. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, path, old_string, new_string.
PATCH MODE (mode='patch'): apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, patch.

mode.description: Edit mode. 'replace' (default): requires path + old_string + new_string. 'patch': requires patch content only.

path.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. File path to edit.

old_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include surrounding context lines to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Replacement text. Pass empty string '' to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
REQUIRED when mode='patch'. V4A format patch content. Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

# Nexa Paraguay — AI Agent Guide

**Live:** https://nexa.paragu-ai.com  
**Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay  
**Docker service:** nexa_web (1 replica)  
**Local dev:** `http://localhost:3000`  

## Core Architecture

- **Framework:** Next.js 16 App Router (React 19, TypeScript 5)
- **Content:** Purely file-based — `content/{locale}.json`, `nexa-pages/{slug}.json`, `images.json`, `site.json`, `testimonials.json`
- **No database:** All content loads from JSON files via `src/lib/page-data.ts` (no Supabase)
- **Loading:** In-memory LRU cache with 30s TTL
- **Section overrides:** Component overrides live in `src/components/` and are registered in `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx`
- **Locales:** 4 complete — ES, EN, NL, DE (all 34 sections, all properly translated)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4 with `@theme` tokens in `src/app/globals.css`

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `content/{locale}.json` | All text content per locale (34 sections each) |
| `nexa-pages/{slug}.json` | Page configs — section ordering + content key references |
| `images.json` | Image manifest — 82+ entries across 14 categories |
| `site.json` | Domain, features, booking URL, social links |
| `src/lib/page-data.ts` | File-based content loader (no Supabase) |
| `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx` | Section registry with overrides |
| `src/components/ProcessSection.tsx` | Timeline component override |
| `src/components/TeamSection.tsx` | Team cards override |
| `src/components/StorySection.tsx` | Story layout override |
| `src/components/PageHeroSection.tsx` | Hero section override |
| `src/components/CtaBanner.tsx` | CTA banner override |
| `src/components/BlogSection.tsx` | Blog grid override |
| `src/components/FaqSection.tsx` | FAQ with categories override |
| `src/components/BookingEmbedSection.tsx` | Booking section override |
| `src/components/ContactDetailsSection.tsx` | Contact card override |
| `src/components/ExitPopup.tsx` | Exit popup (strings from content JSON) |

## Build & Deploy

### Local Development
```bash
npm run dev          # Dev server with Turbopack (compiles in ~350ms)
npm run build        # Production build
npm run start        # Production server
```

### Docker Deploy (VPS)
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod --no-cache .
docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web
```

### Screenshots
```bash
npm run screenshots  # Captures 22 pages

{"content": "     1|import { LOCALES, DEFAULT_LOCALE, LOCALE_COOKIE, resolveLocale } from '@ai-whisperers/i18n'
     2|import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
     3|
     4|function getLocale(request: NextRequest): string {
     5|  const pathname = request.nextUrl.pathname
     6|  const segments = pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean)
     7|  if (segments.length > 0 && (LOCALES as readonly string[]).includes(segments[0])) return segments[0]
     8|  const cookie = request.cookies.get(LOCALE_COOKIE)?.value
     9|  if (cookie && (LOCALES as readonly string[]).includes(cookie)) return cookie
    10|  return DEFAULT_LOCALE
    11|}
    12|
    13|export function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
    14|  const pathname = request.nextUrl.pathname
    15|  if (pathname.startsWith('/_next') || pathname.startsWith('/api') || pathname.startsWith('/images') || pathname.startsWith('/fonts') || pathname === '/favicon.ico' || pathname.includes('.')) return NextResponse.next()
    16|  const segments = pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean)
    17|  if (segments.length > 0 && (LOCALES as readonly string[]).includes(segments[0])) return NextResponse.next()
    18|  const locale = getLocale(request)
    19|  const url = new URL(`/${locale}${pathname === '/' ? '' : pathname}`, request.url)
    20|  const res = NextResponse.redirect(url)
    21|  res.cookies.set(LOCALE_COOKIE, locale, { maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 365 })
    22|  return res
    23|}
    24|
    25|export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next|api|images|fonts|favicon).*)'] }

{"content": "     1|import { readFileSync, existsSync, readdirSync } from 'fs'
     2|import { join } from 'path'
     3|
     4|const REPO = process.cwd()
     5|
     6|interface CacheEntry { data: any; timestamp: number }
     7|const cache = new Map<string, CacheEntry>()
     8|const TTL = 30_000
     9|
    10|function getCached(key: string): any | null {
    11|  const entry = cache.get(key)
    12|  if (entry && (Date.now() - entry.timestamp) < TTL) return entry.data
    13|  return null
    14|}
    15|
    16|function setCache(key: string, data: any) {
    17|  cache.set(key, { data, timestamp: Date.now() })
    18|  if (cache.size > 20) {
    19|    const entries = [...cache.entries()].sort((a, b) => a[1].timestamp - b[1].timestamp)
    20|    for (const [key] of entries.slice(0, cache.size - 20)) cache.delete(key)
    21|  }
    22|}
    23|
    24|function loadJson<T>(...pathSegments: string[]): T | null {
    25|  try {
    26|    return JSON.parse(readFileSync(join(REPO, ...pathSegments), 'utf-8'))
    27|  } catch {
    28|    return null
    29|  }
    30|}
    31|
    32|export async function loadPageData(locale: string, slug: string): Promise<any> {
    33|  const cacheKey = `page:${locale}:${slug}`
    34|  const cached = getCached(cacheKey)
    35|  if (cached) return cached
    36|
    37|  const content = loadJson<Record<string, any>>('content', `${locale}.json`)
    38|  if (!content) return null
    39|
    40|  const pageConfig = loadJson<any>('nexa-pages', `${slug}.json`)
    41|  const images = loadJson<any>('images.json')
    42|  const testimonials = loadJson<any>('testimonials.json')
    43|
    44|  if (testimonials?.testimonials?.length) {
    45|    content.testimonials = testimonials.testimonials
    46|  }
    47|
    48|  const result = { content, pageConfig, images, pageId: slug, locale }
    49|  setCache(cacheKey, result)
    50|  return result
    51|}
    52|
    53|export async function loadBlogPost(locale: string, slug: string): Promise<any> {
    54|  const cacheKey = `blog:${locale}:${slug}`
    55|  const cached = getCached(cacheKey)
    56|  if (cached) return cached
    57|
    58|  const content = loadJson<Record<string, any>>('content', `${locale}.json`)
    59|  if (!content) return null
    60|
    61|  const posts = loadJson<any>('content', 'blog', `posts-${locale}.json`)
    62|    || loadJson<any>('content', 'blog', 'posts.json')
    63|  if (!posts) return null
    64|
    65|  const list = posts.posts || posts
    66|  const post = Array.isArray(list) ? list.find((p: any) => p.slug === slug) : null
    67|  if (!post) return null
    68|
    69|  const result = { content, locale, post }
    70|  setCache(cacheKey, result)
    71|  return result
    72|}
    73|
    74|export function getPageSlugs(): string[] {
    75|  const pagesDir = join(REPO, 'nexa-pages')
    76|  if (!existsSync(pagesDir)) return []
    77|  return readdirSync(pagesDir).filter((f) => f.endsWith('.json')).map((f) => f.replace('.json', ''))
    78|}
    79|
    80|export function getBlogSlugs(locale: string): string[] {
    81|  const path = join(REPO, 'content', 'blog', `posts-${locale}.json`)
    82|  const fallback = join(REPO, 'content', 'blog', 'posts.json')
    83|  const target = existsSync(path) ? path : (existsSync(fallback) ? fallback : null)
    84|  if (!target) return []
    85|  const posts = loadJson<any>(...target.replace(REPO + '/', '').split('/'))
    86|  if (!posts) return []
    87|  return (posts.posts || posts).filter((p: any) => p.slug).map((p: any) => p.slug)
    88|}

{"content": "     1|# Nexa Paraguay — AI Agent Guide
     2|
     3|**Live:** https://nexa.paragu-ai.com  
     4|**Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay  
     5|**Docker service:** nexa_web (1 replica)  
     6|**Local dev:** `http://localhost:3000`  
     7|
     8|## Core Architecture
     9|
    10|- **Framework:** Next.js 16 App Router (React 19, TypeScript 5)
    11|- **Content:** Purely file-based — `content/{locale}.json`, `nexa-pages/{slug}.json`, `images.json`, `site.json`, `testimonials.json`
    12|- **No database:** All content loads from JSON files via `src/lib/page-data.ts` (no Supabase)
    13|- **Loading:** In-memory LRU cache with 30s TTL
    14|- **Section overrides:** Component overrides live in `src/components/` and are registered in `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx`
    15|- **Locales:** 4 complete — ES, EN, NL, DE (all 34 sections, all properly translated)
    16|- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4 with `@theme` tokens in `src/app/globals.css`
    17|
    18|## Key Files
    19|
    20|| File | Purpose |
    21||------|---------|
    22|| `content/{locale}.json` | All text content per locale (34 sections each) |
    23|| `nexa-pages/{slug}.json` | Page configs — section ordering + content key references |
    24|| `images.json` | Image manifest — 82+ entries across 14 categories |
    25|| `site.json` | Domain, features, booking URL, social links |
    26|| `src/lib/page-data.ts` | File-based content loader (no Supabase) |
    27|| `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx` | Section registry with overrides |
    28|| `src/components/ProcessSection.tsx` | Timeline component override |
    29|| `src/components/TeamSection.tsx` | Team cards override |
    30|| `src/components/StorySection.tsx` | Story layout override |
    31|| `src/components/PageHeroSection.tsx` | Hero section override |
    32|| `src/components/CtaBanner.tsx` | CTA banner override |
    33|| `src/components/BlogSection.tsx` | Blog grid override |
    34|| `src/components/FaqSection.tsx` | FAQ with categories override |
    35|| `src/components/BookingEmbedSection.tsx` | Booking section override |
    36|| `src/components/ContactDetailsSection.tsx` | Contact card override |
    37|| `src/components/ExitPopup.tsx` | Exit popup (strings from content JSON) |
    38|
    39|## Build & Deploy
    40|
    41|### Local Development
    42|```bash
    43|npm run dev          # Dev server with Turbopack (compiles in ~350ms)
    44|npm run build        # Production build
    45|npm run start        # Production server
    46|```
    47|
    48|### Docker Deploy (VPS)
    49|```bash
    50|npm run build
    51|docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod --no-cache .
    52|docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web
    53|```
    54|
    55|### Screenshots
    56|```bash
    57|npm run screenshots  # Captures 22 pages
    58|```
    59|
    60|## Documentation
    61|
    62|| Category | Key Files |
    63||----------|-----------|
    64|| **Client Profile** | `docs/01-client/sonia-weiss-complete-profile.md`, `docs/01-client/client-intelligence.json` |
    65|| **Services Matrix** | `docs/01-client/services-opportunities-matrix.md` (210 items) |
    66|| **Pricing** | `docs/pricing-matrix-complete.md` (32 services, 12 competitors) |
    67|| **Asunción Data** | `docs/asuncion-complete-1485-places.csv` (1,485 places with GPS) |
    68|| **Full Report** | `docs/nexa-complete-intelligence-report.md` |
    69|| **Location Intel** | `docs/central-asuncion-complete-analysis.md` |
    70|| **Department Analysis** | `docs/complete-paraguay-department-analysis.md` |
    71|| **Location DB** | `docs/paraguay-locations-database.json` (1,548 nationwide) |
    72|| **Meeting Prep** | `docs/01-client/meeting-may-11-questions.md` (51 questions) |
    73|| **Deployment** | `docs/10-deployment/deployment-runbook.md` |
    74|| **Launch** | `docs/11-launch/launch-runbook.md` |
    75|| **Full Index** | `docs/README.md` |
    76|
    77|## Client: Sonia Weiss
    78|
    79|| Field | Value |
    80||-------|-------|
    81|| Known as | Sonia Weiss (tax: Sonia Edith López Charotti Ramírez) |
    82|| Phone | [REDACTED_PHONE] |
    83|| Languages | Spanish, Dutch (7 yrs), English, Guarani |

    86|| Children | Iván (you), Kiki, Luana |

    90|## Critical Notes

    92|- **Pricing AMBIGUITY:** Sonia said "$1,500 todo incluido" in audio. Our model says $2,900-$6,900. Most likely: $1,500 her fee + ~$1,400 costs = $2,900 total. **Must resolve with Sonia.**

     2|on:
     3|  push:
     4|    branches: [main]
     5|  workflow_dispatch:
     6|
     7|env:
     8|  REGISTRY: ghcr.io/ai-whisperers
     9|  IMAGE: nexa-paraguay
    10|  SERVICE: nexa_web
    11|
    12|jobs:
    13|  build:
    14|    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    15|    steps:
    16|      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    17|      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    18|        with: { node-version: 22, cache: 'npm' }
    19|      - run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps
    20|      - run: npm run build
    21|      - run: npm test || echo "Tests skipped (no Playwright)"
    22|      - name: Build Docker
    23|        run: docker build -t $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:latest -t $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }} .
    24|
    25|      - name: Push to registry
    26|        run: |
    27|          echo "${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" | docker login ghcr.io -u ${{ github.actor }} --password-stdin
    28|          docker push $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:latest
    29|          docker push $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }}
    30|
    31|      - name: Deploy to VPS (staging)
    32|        run: |
    33|          ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no [REDACTED_EMAIL] \
    34|            "docker service update --image $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }} ${SERVICE}-staging"
    35|
    36|      - name: Visual regression check
    37|        run: |
    38|          sleep 15
    39|          curl -sI https://staging.nexa.paragu-ai.com | head -5
    40|
    41|      - name: Promote to production
    42|        if: success()
    43|        run: |
    44|          ssh [REDACTED_EMAIL] \
    45|            "docker service update --image $REGISTRY/$IMAGE:${{ github.sha }} $SERVICE"
    46|
    47|      - name: Health check
    48|        run: |
    49|          for i in 1 2 3; do
    50|            sleep 10
    51|            STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://nexa.paragu-ai.com)
    52|            if [ "$STATUS" = "200" ]; then echo "OK"; exit 0; fi
    53|          done
    54|          echo "FAILED after 3 retries"; exit 1
    55|
    56|      - name: Notify on failure
    57|        if: failure()
    58|        run: |
    59|          curl -s -X POST "${{ secrets.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL }}" \
    60|            -d "chat_id=${{ secrets.TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID }}" \
    61|            -d "text=Nexa deploy FAILED (${{ github.sha }}). Check: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions"

     2|on:
     3|  push:
     4|    branches: [main]
     5|  workflow_dispatch:
     6|
     7|jobs:
     8|  screenshot-diff:
     9|    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    10|    steps:
    11|      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    12|      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    13|        with:
    14|          node-version: 20
    15|          cache: 'npm'
    16|      - run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps
    17|      - name: Install Playwright
    18|        run: npx playwright install chromium --with-deps
    19|      - name: Build & Screenshot
    20|        run: node scripts/screenshot-all.mjs
    21|        timeout-minutes: 10
    22|      - name: Store screenshots
    23|        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
    24|        with:
    25|          name: screenshots
    26|          path: screenshots/
    27|          retention-days: 30
    28|      - name: Compare with baseline
    29|        run: |
    30|          BASELINE_DIR=".screenshot-baselines"
    31|          NEWEST=$(ls -t screenshots/ 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    32|          if [ -z "$NEWEST" ]; then
    33|            echo "No screenshots generated"
    34|            exit 1
    35|          fi
    36|          mkdir -p "$BASELINE_DIR"
    37|          cp -r "screenshots/$NEWEST" "$BASELINE_DIR/$NEWEST"
    38|          git config user.name "nexa-bot"
    39|          git config user.email "[REDACTED_EMAIL]"
    40|          git add "$BASELINE_DIR/"
    41|          git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "chore: update screenshot baseline $NEWEST"
    42|          git push
    43|        env:
